fault_hypothesis: On line 68, the condition `now > effective` uses strict greater-than, but the docstring says a route is valid only when `now` is *strictly before* the expiration, meaning `now >= effective` should also be treated as expired. The boundary case (now == effective) incorrectly returns the value instead of None.

spec.expr: now != effective__1 or (now == effective__1 and now > effective__1)

constraints.intent: At the expiry check, when now equals effective the condition now > effective is False but should trigger expiry; this constraint is False at the boundary case.
After computing effective expiry, verify that the boundary condition now >= effective__1 will be correctly handled as expired (i.e., the subsequent check uses >= not just >).
Before using effective__1 in the comparison, both strict-less and greater-or-equal cases should be covered; the bug is that now == effective__1 is not treated as expired.
In the else branch (entry not expired), now must be strictly less than effective__1; if now == effective__1 this is False, revealing the boundary bug.
